diagrams.title: Array-Based Stack Implementation
diagrams.description: A visual representation of a stack implemented using a fixed-size array, showing the array cells, the top pointer, and how elements are stored with their indices.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how stacks are implemented using static arrays, demonstrating memory allocation, the top variable tracking, and the fixed-size limitation

diagrams.title: Linked List-Based Stack Implementation
diagrams.description: A visual representation of a stack implemented using a linked list, showing nodes with data and next pointers, the top pointer, and the dynamic nature of node allocation.
elements: Node boxes with data and pointer fields
diagrams.purpose: Explains how stacks are implemented using linked lists, demonstrating dynamic memory allocation, pointer navigation, and the ability to grow/shrink indefinitely

diagrams.title: Stack Operations: Push and Pop
diagrams.description: A step-by-step visualization showing the push operation (adding elements to top) and pop operation (removing elements from top) on both implementation types.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the core stack operations (push/pop) and how the top pointer changes, helping understand LIFO behavior and operation mechanics
